source.section: 3 - AN/AAQ-28(V) LITENING II Targeting Pod > 3.8 - Air-to-Air Operation > 3.8.2 - Point Track

steps.action: Make sure the targeting pod is powered
Press the A/A Master Mode button. The RDR ATTK (radar) page will appear on the right DDI and the FLIR page will appear on the left DDI.
Select FLIR by pressing the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the DDI with the FLIR page (left).
The Select Focus Diamond indicates what display is selected.
Select VVSLV by selecting the VVSLV pushbutton on the FLIR page. The FLIR will be slaved to the line of sight to the velocity vector.
Press OSB next to RTCL (boxed) to display the targeting pod’s reticle
Fly the aircraft to put the velocity vector in the vicinity of the aircraft.
You can zoom and choose desired FLIR/CCD (TV) setting using the appropriate Option Select Buttons on the FLIR page.
Press/bump the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the FLIR format page (left) to attempt to Point Track the target.
When Point Track is obtained, a designation box will appear on the HUD and the targeting pod will track the target.
If you want to “dump” the target, press the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the DDI with the FLIR page (left).
Make sure the radar is powered
Make sure Point Track is obtained
On FLIR page, press OSB next to SLAVE to slave the radar to the targeting pod’s point track.
Both the radar and the TGP will now track the target.
